appliedRows.title: What is an AI anyway? | Mustaf… - TED Talks Daily - Apple Podcasts
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: What is an AI anyway? | Mustafa Suleyman When it comes to artificial intelligence, what are we actually creating? ... says Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman Selection reason: Apple Podcasts页面明确显示该TED演讲的播客版本，标题和描述均包含Mustafa Suleyman，证明其参与。 Source preview: TED Talks Daily What is an AI anyway? | Mustafa Suleyman When it comes to artificial intelligence, what are we actually creating? Even those closest to its development are struggling to describe exactly where things are headed, says Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman, one of the primary architects of the AI models many of us use today. He offers an honest and compelling new vision for the future of AI, proposing an unignorable metaphor — a new digital species — to focus attention on this extraordinary moment. (Followed by a Q&A with head of TED Chris Anderson) Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information. Hosts & Guests Elise Hu Elise Hu Host Mustafa Suleyman Guest Information... appliedRows.title: Percy Liang - Stanford Computer Science
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: I am interested in fundamental questions around learning and intelligence... I lead Marin to build models openly. Selection reason: 个人学术主页，详细说明其研究兴趣（包括鲁棒性）和领导项目，是权威的一手来源。 Source preview: Percy Liang Image 1Percy Liang Professor of Computer Science (and courtesy in Statistics) Gates 366 ( Research I am drawn to simple things, want to understand things deeply, and like to build useful systems. I am interested in fundamental questions around learning and intelligence. How well can we learn in the data-limited, infinite compute regime? How do we model interestingness and curiosity? How can we push the frontier of human knowledge? I lead Marin to build models openly. Here is a talk I gave about it. We practice a radical form of openness called open development, which goes beyond open-weight and open-source: Marin experiments (both successful and failed) are preregistered and liv... appliedRows.title: Percy Liang - Stanford Profiles
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Percy Liang is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University... and the director of the Center for Research on Foundation Models (CRFM). Selection reason: 斯坦福大学官方个人资料页，明确列出其职位、所属机构及研究方向，是权威且直接的来源。 Source preview: Percy Liang Percy Liang Professor of Computer Science and Senior Fellow at the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI Bio Percy Liang is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University (B.S. from MIT, 2004; Ph.D. from UC Berkeley, 2011) and the director of the Center for Research on Foundation Models (CRFM). He is currently focused on making foundation models (in particular, language models) more accessible through open-source and understandable through rigorous benchmarking. In the past, he has worked on many topics centered on machine learning and natural language processing, including robustness, interpretability, human interaction, learning theory, grounding, semanti... appliedRows.title: Sam Altman May Control Our Future—Can He Be Trusted?
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: A Reporter at Large Sam Altman May Control Our Future—Can He Be Trusted? New interviews and closely guarded documents shed light on the persistent doubts about the head of OpenAI. Selection reason: 权威媒体深度调查报道，直接探讨Sam Altman的可信度与OpenAI治理问题，与原始标题主题高度相关，可作为权威补充。 Source preview: A Reporter at Large Sam Altman May Control Our Future—Can He Be Trusted? New interviews and closely guarded documents shed light on the persistent doubts about the head of OpenAI. By Ronan Farrow and Andrew Marantz Altman promised to be a safe steward for A.I. But some of his colleagues believed that he was not trustworthy enough to, as one put it, “have his finger on the button.”Visual by David Szauder; Generated using A.I. In the fall of 2023, Ilya Sutskever, OpenAI’s chief scientist, sent secret memos to three fellow-members of the organization’s board of directors. For weeks, they’d been having furtive discussions about whether Sam Altman, OpenAI’s C.E.O., and Greg Brockman, his second-... Selection reason: Apple Podcasts页面明确显示播客标题、嘉宾姓名、职位及讨论主题，直接证明该内容与人物相关。 Source preview: Gradient Dissent: Conversations on AI Wojciech Zaremba — What Could Make AI Conscious? Wojciech joins us to talk the principles behind OpenAI, the Fermi Paradox, and the future stages of developments in AGI. Wojciech Zaremba is a co-founder of OpenAI, a research company dedicated to discovering and enacting the path to safe artificial general intelligence. He was also Head of Robotics, where his team developed general-purpose robots through new approaches to transfer learning, and taught robots complex behaviors. Connect with Wojciech: Personal website: Twitter: Topics Discussed: 0:00 Sneak peek and intro 1:03 The people and principles behind OpenAI 6:31 The stages of future AI developments... appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Yann LeCun is VP & Chief AI Scientist at Meta and Silver Professor at NYU affiliated with the Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences & the Center for Data Science. He was the founding Director of FAIR and of the NYU Center for Data Science. Selection reason: 播客官方页面，明确包含Yann LeCun的职位、背景及播客主题，是原始来源的权威替代。 Source preview: View All 20VC: Yann LeCun on Why Artificial Intelligence Will Not Dominate Humanity, Why No Economists Believe All Jobs Will Be Replaced by AI, Why the Size of Models Matters Less and Less & Why Open Models Beat Closed Models May 14, 2023 Posted by Yann LeCun is VP & Chief AI Scientist at Meta and Silver Professor at NYU affiliated with the Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences & the Center for Data Science. He was the founding Director of FAIR and of the NYU Center for Data Science. After a postdoc in Toronto he joined AT&T Bell Labs in 1988, and AT&T Labs in 1996 as Head of Image Processing Research. He joined NYU as a professor in 2003 and Meta/Facebook in 2013. He is the recipient ... appliedRows.title: Joelle Pineau – CIFAR
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Joëlle Pineau is a Professor and William Dawson Scholar at the School of Computer Science at McGill University... She is a core academic member of Mila and a Canada CIFAR AI Chair. Selection reason: CIFAR（加拿大高等研究院）官方简介，确认其作为加拿大CIFAR AI Chair的身份和研究方向。 Source preview: CIFAR header logo menu_mobile_logo_alt Follow Us post_content Joelle Pineau Appointment Canada CIFAR AI Chair Pan-Canadian AI Strategy Connect McGill University Google Scholar About Appointed Canada CIFAR AI Chair – 2018 Renewed Canada CIFAR AI Chair – 2023 Joëlle Pineau is a Professor and William Dawson Scholar at the School of Computer Science at McGill University, where she co-directs the Reasoning and Learning Lab. She is a core academic member of Mila and a Canada CIFAR AI Chair. Pineau’s research focuses on developing new models and algorithms for planning and learning in complex partially-observable domains. She also works on applying these algorithms to complex problems in robotics,... appliedRows.title: [PDF] Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners | OpenAI
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners Alec Radford 1 Jeffrey Wu 1 Rewon Child 1 David Luan 1 Dario Amodei 1 Ilya Sutskever 1 Selection reason: OpenAI官方发布的GPT-2论文PDF，作者列表明确包含亚历克·拉德福德，是证明其贡献的权威原始来源。 Source preview: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners Alec Radford 1 Jeffrey Wu 1 Rewon Child 1 David Luan 1 Dario Amodei 1 Ilya Sutskever 1 Abstract Natural language processing tasks, such as ques-tion answering, machine translation, reading com-prehension, and summarization, are typically approached with supervised learning on task-speciﬁc datasets. We demonstrate that language models begin to learn these tasks without any ex-plicit supervision when trained on a new dataset of millions of webpages called WebText. When conditioned on a document plus questions, the an-swers generated by the language model reach 55 F1 on the CoQA dataset - matching or exceeding the performance of 3 out of 4 ba... MiMo rationale: 候选来源中，OpenAI官方PDF是唯一权威且直接的原始来源，明确列出了亚历克·拉德福德作为作者，完美满足证据要求。其他来源要么无关，要么是二手或非权威页面，不适合作为替换来源。 Evidence requirements: Source must name 亚历克·拉德福德 directly. | Source must support why "‪Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners‬" belongs on this person's page, or provide a better authoritative replacement. | Prefer official profile, institution/company page, paper detail page, author page, interview transcript, or reliable media with clear attribution. | Do not use search pages, login walls, empty captures, generic product pages without person role, or social snippets without context as replace_source.
